# chimpbase

> Action-driven backend runtime monorepo for Bun and Deno. Postgres-first in production, SQLite or memory for local work, with a small explicit TypeScript runtime surface built around `action`, `subscription`, `worker`, `cron`, and `workflow`.

## Docs

- [README.md](README.md): Primary monorepo overview, quick start, runtime primitives, telemetry persistence, workflows, examples, and release model.
- [packages/bun/README.md](packages/bun/README.md): Bun host usage, runtime model, and Bun-first examples.
- [packages/runtime/README.md](packages/runtime/README.md): Public DSL for `action`, `cron`, `subscription`, `worker`, and `workflow`.
- [packages/core/README.md](packages/core/README.md): Low-level engine and host-facing internals.

## Packages

- [packages/bun](packages/bun): Bun host package and CLI. Exports `createChimpbase`, `createChimpbase.from(...)`, app loading helpers, and schema/workflow sync helpers.
- [packages/deno](packages/deno): Deno CLI/self-hosted host package with the same runtime DSL.
- [packages/runtime](packages/runtime): Public runtime DSL, context types, and workflow contracts used by application code.
- [packages/core](packages/core): Engine, registry contracts, orchestration internals, and telemetry buffering/persistence internals.
- [packages/postgres](packages/postgres): Reusable Postgres adapter used by hosts.
- [packages/tooling](packages/tooling): Project loading, config helpers, schema sync, workflow contract sync, migrations, and CLI support.
- [packages/otel](packages/otel): Optional OpenTelemetry integration. Exports `createOtelSink()` which implements `ChimpbaseTelemetrySink` to bridge `ctx.log`, `ctx.metric`, and `ctx.trace` to OTel providers.

## Examples

- [examples/bun/todo-ts](examples/bun/todo-ts): Canonical plain TypeScript example and the best starting point for understanding the intended app shape.
- [examples/bun/todo-ts-decorators](examples/bun/todo-ts-decorators): Decorators-based example.
- [examples/bun/todo-ts-nestjs](examples/bun/todo-ts-nestjs): NestJS integration example.
- [examples/bun/todo-ts-nestjs-decorators](examples/bun/todo-ts-nestjs-decorators): NestJS plus decorators example.
- [examples/bun/factory-controller](examples/bun/factory-controller): Multi-replica Docker Compose example. Uses all primitives (actions, subscriptions, workers, queues, cron, KV, collections, streams, telemetry) with 3 replicas coordinating through PostgreSQL.

## Key Concepts

- Prefer Postgres in production. SQLite and memory are supported for local development and tests.
- The main DSL primitives are `action`, `subscription`, `worker`, `cron`, and `workflow`.
- Common runtime context helpers are `ctx.db.query(...)`, `ctx.db.kysely<TDatabase>()`, `ctx.pubsub.publish(...)`, `ctx.queue.enqueue(...)`, `ctx.kv`, `ctx.collection`, `ctx.stream`, `ctx.workflow`, `ctx.secret`, `ctx.log`, `ctx.metric`, `ctx.trace`, and `ctx.action(...)`.
- `subscription(...)` can be marked `idempotent: true` with a stable `name` to deduplicate cross-process delivery.
- `queue.enqueue(...)` plus `worker(...)` is the durable background job model.
- `cron(...)` uses standard 5-field UTC cron expressions and runs through the same worker execution path.
- `workflow(...)` is for long-running stateful processes that must survive restarts, time delays, and external signals.
- Telemetry persistence is optional and can write logs, metrics, and traces to internal streams.
- Telemetry sinks (`ChimpbaseTelemetrySink`) allow exporting `ctx.log`, `ctx.metric`, and `ctx.trace` to external systems. Pass `sinks: [createOtelSink()]` to `createChimpbase()` for OpenTelemetry export.
- Multiple containers running the same `chimpbase.app.ts` can share a single PostgreSQL database. Queues use row-level locking, cron fires once per slot regardless of replica count, and idempotent subscriptions deduplicate across processes.
- Published packages currently ship TypeScript source, not prebuilt `dist/` artifacts.

## Runtime Surface

- `ChimpbaseContext` exposes raw SQL via `ctx.db.query(...)` and typed Kysely access via `ctx.db.kysely<TDatabase>()`.
- `ctx.db.kysely().transaction()` is intentionally unsupported; actions, subscriptions, and queue handlers already run inside runtime-managed transactions.
- Workflow operations used throughout the repo are `ctx.workflow.start(...)`, `ctx.workflow.signal(...)`, and `ctx.workflow.get(...)`.
- In-memory telemetry is available through `drainTelemetryRecords()` on the host.
- When telemetry persistence is enabled, the internal streams are `_chimpbase.logs`, `_chimpbase.metrics`, and `_chimpbase.traces`.
- Decorator-based composition exists for `Action`, `Subscription`, `Worker`, and `Cron`, and apps can collect those registrations with `registrationsFrom(...)`.

## Key Entry Points

- [examples/bun/todo-ts/chimpbase.app.ts](examples/bun/todo-ts/chimpbase.app.ts): Canonical explicit app composition using a plain object with `registrations`.
- [examples/bun/todo-ts/chimpbase.migrations.ts](examples/bun/todo-ts/chimpbase.migrations.ts): Example migrations definition.
- [examples/bun/todo-ts-decorators/chimpbase.app.ts](examples/bun/todo-ts-decorators/chimpbase.app.ts): Decorators-based app composition using `registrationsFrom(...)`.
- [examples/bun/todo-ts-decorators/src/modules/todos/todo.module.ts](examples/bun/todo-ts-decorators/src/modules/todos/todo.module.ts): Concrete example of `Action`, `Subscription`, and `Worker` decorators in application code.
- [packages/bun/src/library.ts](packages/bun/src/library.ts): Bun public API, project loading helpers, action execution, startup helpers, and schema/workflow sync functions.
- [packages/deno/src/library.ts](packages/deno/src/library.ts): Deno public API mirroring the Bun host shape.
- [packages/runtime/index.ts](packages/runtime/index.ts): Public runtime types and DSL surface.
- [packages/tooling/src/cli.ts](packages/tooling/src/cli.ts): Shared CLI behavior for `dev`, `contracts`, `schema generate`, and `schema check`.

## Development Commands

- `bun install`
- `bun test`
- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run ci:check`
- `bun run dev:todo-ts`
- `bun run --filter @chimpbase/example-todo-ts action -- seedDemoWorkspace '[]'`

## Notes For LLMs And Contributors

- Install dependencies once at the repository root. Examples use Bun workspaces and shared root dependencies.
- Prefer the explicit `chimpbase.app.ts` composition shape for multi-module apps.
- Compatibility helpers like `createChimpbase.from(...)` and `createChimpbaseDeno.from(...)` still exist, but project loading prefers `chimpbase.app.ts` when it is present.
- If you need the runtime API shape, trust [packages/runtime/index.ts](packages/runtime/index.ts) and the tests over inferred framework conventions.
- Use the root [tests](tests) directory for runtime, storage, telemetry, event bus, and workflow regression coverage.
- Use each example's local `tests/` directory for application-level patterns and end-to-end flows.
